name: Panoramic Hillsides Trail
name_original: Boucle entre le parc de l'Hermitage et le parc Palmer
description: Hike a moderate 7.0-mile (11.3 km) loop through Parc des Coteaux, passing Hermitage Park and the Jacques Chaban-Delmas Bridge.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around the Garonne-Kanal offer diverse landscapes, from serene canal towpaths shaded by plane trees to picturesque riverside views of the Garonne River. The region features natural settings like the Montech Forest, vineyards, and pine forests, alongside natural reserves and lakeside areas. Hikers can explore a blend of tranquil waterside paths and charming towns, with routes generally characterized by flat, accessible terrain. Best easy hiking trails around the Garonne-Kanal The most popular easy hiking route is Bordeaux: Two Bridges Trail, a 4.6 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete, offering urban exploration along the Garonne River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Via Sancti Martini - Saragosse Way Trail, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This route provides a gentle walk through varied landscapes, characteristic of historical pilgrimage routes. Local hikers also love the Witch's Route of Tredos from Salardu — Valle de Aran, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through varied natural terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. Hiking around the Garonne-Kanal is defined by canal towpaths, lush forests, and riverside views. The network offers options for various ability levels, with many routes being flat and accessible. The routes in Garonne canal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2400 reviews. More than 22000 hikers have used komoot to explore Garonne canal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around the Garonne Canal?
A: The Garonne Canal region offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with over 540 routes specifically categorized as easy. In total, there are more than 1000 hiking routes available, catering to various preferences and abilities.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for easy hikes along the Garonne Canal?
A: Easy hikes along the Garonne Canal are generally characterized by flat, accessible terrain. Many routes follow serene canal towpaths, often shaded by majestic plane trees, and offer picturesque riverside views of the Garonne River. These paths are well-developed greenways, ideal for relaxed strolls away from traffic.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on easy hikes along the Garonne Canal?
A: Easy hikes along the Garonne Canal showcase diverse natural settings. You can expect to see lush greenery, dense areas like the Montech Forest, and picturesque riverside views. Some routes also lead through vineyards, pine forests, natural reserves, and lakeside areas such as the Lake of Bordeaux.

Q: Are there any historical or engineering marvels to discover on easy walks?
A: Yes, the Garonne Canal is rich in historical and engineering marvels. You can encounter numerous historic locks and significant aqueducts, such as those crossing the Tarn near Moissac and the Garonne at Agen. While currently out of service, the unique Montech Water Slope is also a notable feature along the canal.

Q: Can I find any castles or historical sites near the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, there are several historical sites and castles near the easy hiking trails. For example, you might explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1025678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château du Hâ&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1719868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Palaminy&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features charming historic towns like Auvillar, known for its circular grain market, and Nérac with its quaint winding streets.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails around the Garonne Canal suitable for families with children?
A: Absolutely. Many of the easy hiking trails along the Garonne Canal are perfect for families with children due to their flat, accessible nature and well-maintained paths. The greenways provide a safe and enjoyable environment for all ages, often passing through charming towns or natural areas.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the easy hiking trails along the Garonne Canal?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on the easy hiking trails along the Garonne Canal, especially on the towpaths and greenways. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash and be mindful of local regulations, particularly in nature reserves or specific park areas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in the Garonne Canal region?
A: The easy trails in the Garonne Canal region are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2400 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil waterside paths, the lush scenery, and the accessibility of the routes, making for a consistently positive experience.

Q: Are there any easy circular hiking routes along the Garonne Canal?
A: Yes, the Garonne Canal region offers several easy circular hiking routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36761051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dijeaux Gate – Saint-Éloi Church loop from Quinconces&lt;/a&gt; in Bordeaux is a popular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10534704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portet-sur-Garonne ferry – Ornithological observatory loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering a pleasant round trip.

Q: What are some recommended easy hikes under 5km around the Garonne Canal?
A: For shorter, easy walks under 5km, you can consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932127572/witch-s-route-of-tredos-from-salardu-valle-de-aran" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Witch's Route of Tredos from Salardu — Valle de Aran&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.5 km. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e838344332/amadour-way-stage-7" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadour Way - Stage 7&lt;/a&gt;, approximately 4.1 km, offering a gentle walk.

Q: Where can I find parking for easy hikes along the Garonne Canal?
A: Parking availability varies along the Garonne Canal. Many towns and villages situated along the canal, such as Bordeaux, Moissac, and Nérac, offer public parking facilities. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check local maps or route descriptions for designated parking areas, often found near canal access points or town centers.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, the Garonne Canal meanders past numerous charming towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities. Places like Bordeaux, Buzet-sur-Baïsse, Auvillar, and Moissac offer various options for refreshments and meals, often conveniently located near the canal paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to enjoy easy hikes along the Garonne Canal?
A: The Garonne Canal is enjoyable for easy hikes throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, but many towpaths are shaded by plane trees, providing relief. Winter offers a quieter experience, though some amenities might have reduced hours.
tips: A pretty park with a blue lake and a few steps that lead to a view of the whole city of Bordeaux

Magnificent bridge connecting Bacalan to the Brazza quays. It was completed at the end of 2012 and made traffic in Bordeaux more fluid. It offers a magnificent walk between the right bank and the left bank, making a loop of just under seven kilometers between the Pierre bridge and the Chaban-Delmas bridge. It's quite impressive to see it open and close. It’s clearly a must!

The promenades of Bordeaux stretch from the Pont de Pierre bridge to the Bacalan district, along the renovated 18th century façades. Thanks to trams and bike paths, it has become a very popular place and there is no shortage of activities.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Loop between Hermitage Park and Palmer Park?
faqs.answer: This moderate hike typically takes around 3 hours to complete. The total distance is approximately 11.3 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a loop, offering a varied experience through urban green spaces and along the Garonne River. Expect paved paths, riverside promenades, and potentially some unpaved sections within parks. It's generally accessible but offers a good workout.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this route, especially in public parks and along river paths. Please keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas and near wildlife, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal for this hike, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon start is recommended. Winter hikes are also possible, but be prepared for cooler, potentially damp conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or Parc des Coteaux?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this trail or the general areas within Parc des Coteaux. It is a public urban and natural space, freely accessible to all.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers a mix of natural beauty and urban architecture. You'll pass through Hermitage Park, enjoy views of the Garonne, and cross the impressive Jacques Chaban-Delmas Bridge. The Quai de Bachalan also offers scenic riverside sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As this is an urban loop, there are multiple points of access. You can typically find street parking near Hermitage Park or other points along the route. Public transport, such as trams or buses, also provides convenient access to various sections of the trail in Bordeaux.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Super Balade 7 - Balade panoramique des coteaux, La route de la corniche à vélo : Blaye / Bordeaux via Bourg, and the ReVE 4 - Villenave d'Ornon - St Louis de Montferrand, among others.

faqs.question: Is the loop suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, much of this loop is suitable for families. The sections through parks and along the river are generally flat and enjoyable. However, the full 11.3 km distance might be long for very young children, so consider shorter segments or using a stroller on paved parts.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as an urban trail, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and public facilities in the neighborhoods you pass through, particularly near the riverfront and in the vicinity of Hermitage Park. It's always a good idea to carry water and snacks, but opportunities for refreshments are available.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: While primarily an urban and park trail, you might spot common urban birds, ducks along the Garonne, and small mammals within the park areas. The green spaces provide a habitat for local flora and fauna.
